text:
KINGS NEUROSCIENCES CENTRE

Clinic Date: 29 December 2021

Dr Priya Menon
Riverside Medical Practice

12 Wharf Street, London, SE8 3AB
Dear Dr Priya Menon
Andrew Collins, DOB: 21-11-1982, Hospital No: H392774 NHS No. 7483926150
Flat 4, 28 Millbank Court, London, SE1 7QP

I reviewed the above patient in the Neurology Clinic today. Since our last contact he has been using a seizure diary app to log symptoms, rescue medication usage, and potential triggers. He reports generally feeling well and has been attending work full-time. Sleep has been variable due to shift patterns, and he has noted higher stress levels around project deadlines. He denies recent alcohol binges and has maintained hydration and meals as advised. He mentioned occasional brief startles in the mornings over the past few weeks, which he recorded as isolated jerks with no confusion.

Regarding recent events, his seizure diary confirms the following: He did not have seizures for over 6 months, but then reported two generalised tonic-clonic seizures two Fridays ago, each preceded by myoclonic jerks. He described the jerks as brief, occurring in clusters over a few minutes before each convulsion. On both occasions the events occurred shortly after waking. The app entries include witness notes from his partner describing bilateral stiffening followed by rhythmic jerking lasting approximately 90–120 seconds, with a post-ictal phase of about 30 minutes. No injuries were recorded; there was tongue biting on the second event but no incontinence. He did not require emergency services and recovered at home.

Medication adherence is self-reported as high and corroborated by his app’s reminder logs. He remains on Levetiracetam 1500 mg b.d., Lamotrigine 150 mg b.d., and Clobazam 10 mg nocte. He reports mild daytime fatigue and intermittent irritability, which he attributes to work stress; no rash, visual disturbance, or gait issues. He uses a smartwatch linked to the diary for sleep tracking; data around the seizure dates indicate reduced sleep duration and increased sleep fragmentation. He has logged caffeine intake on the preceding days as higher than usual.

Neurological examination today is unremarkable. Vital signs are stable. No focal deficits. We reviewed his safety measures: he continues to avoid heights, swims only with supervision, and has updated his workplace seizure action plan. He is aware of DVLA guidance and has paused driving; we discussed ongoing compliance with statutory requirements.

Plan: I have requested an updated set of routine bloods (including U&E, LFTs, FBC) and a drug level for Lamotrigine to assist with future decision-making. I have also scheduled an EEG within the next 4–6 weeks to capture any interictal changes, and we will review his sleep routine and diary trends at that time. He will continue current medications unchanged for now, and we agreed on close monitoring via the app with prompts to flag any further convulsions, clusters of myoclonic jerks, or injuries. We discussed stress management techniques and regular sleep-wake timing; he is amenable to trialling app-based sleep hygiene prompts. No immediate changes to treatment were made today.

I will review him again in three months or sooner if there is any recurrence or escalation in events. Please feel free to contact me if you require further information.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: He did not have seizures for over 6 months, but then reported two generalised tonic-clonic seizures two Fridays ago, each preceded by myoclonic jerks.
He did not have seizures for over 6 months, but then reported two generalised tonic-clonic seizures two Fridays ago, each preceded by myoclonic jerks

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: From the letter: The patient had no seizures for over 6 months, then had two generalised tonic-clonic seizures on the same day, each preceded by a cluster of myoclonic jerks. Count events over the explicit time window: 2 GTC + 2 myoclonic clusters = 4 seizure events in >6 months. Using the minimum stated window (6 months), normalize to 4 per 6 month. The mention of occasional brief startles over the past few weeks aligns with these clusters and does not provide additional quantifiable events.
seizure_frequency_number: He did not have seizures for over 6 months, but then reported two generalised tonic-clonic seizures two Fridays ago, each preceded by myoclonic jerks.
